reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. The hypothesis ‖z‖ = 1 encodes "point on the unit circle". The partial sum s(n) = ∑_{i ∈ range n} z^(i+1)/(i+1)^2 correctly equals ∑_{k=1}^{n} z^k/k^2, which is the standard indexing for this series (since the k=0 term is undefined). The conclusion ∃ y, Tendsto s atTop (𝓝 y) is the standard Lean/Mathlib way to express "the series converges at z" — i.e., the sequence of partial sums has a limit. No extra hypotheses are added and no hypotheses are dropped. This is a standard, equivalent encoding.
